title: Test execution report
description: Data contract for the test-report format: a test-runner console. The page is a full-width app shell — a top run summary band (run selector + environment chip + result donut + big pass-rate %, totals, duration), a left collapsible suite tree (each suite row carries a slim pass/fail/skip stacked mini-bar), and a center case table for the selected suite (status badge, case name, duration, owner, a last-N-runs sparkline, tags) with status-filter chips, search, and sortable columns. A case row expands into a failure-detail panel (error message, mono stack trace, per-step status, attachment names). Per-run case results live in cases[].results keyed by run id; the donut, pass-rate, suite roll-ups and totals are COMPUTED from the active run's results — never hard-coded. Renders to a self-contained, interactive, themeable, PC-wide HTML console. The sample's embedded #report-data is a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome (org / project / title / theme). Brand-neutral: keep {{ORG}} / {{PROJECT}} / {{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label shown in the summary band, e.g. 'Test execution' or {{REPORT_TYPE}}.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line sub-title / pipeline or service context.

date.description: ISO date the report was generated (YYYY-MM-DD).

theme.description: Initial brand theme; applied as <html data-theme>. Test-report default is 'green'.

runs.description: The execution runs the report can switch between. Ship at least two so the run selector is meaningful. Each run is a distinct pipeline execution; per-case outcomes for the run are stored under cases[].results[run.id].

id.description: Stable run identifier, e.g. 'run-1042'. Used as the key into cases[].results.

label.description: Optional human label shown in the run selector, e.g. 'Run #1042'. Falls back to id.

env.description: Environment / pipeline the run executed in, e.g. 'CI · staging' or 'ap-northeast-1'. Shown as a chip in the summary band.

branch.description: Optional VCS branch or tag the run executed against, e.g. 'release/4.18' or 'main@a1b2c3d'.

startedAt.description: ISO-8601 start timestamp of the run.

finishedAt.description: ISO-8601 finish timestamp of the run.

durationMs.description: Total wall-clock duration of the run in milliseconds (may exceed the sum of case durations due to parallelism).

activeRunId.description: The run.id selected on load. The summary band, donut, pass-rate, suite roll-ups and case statuses all render from this run's results until the user picks another run.

statuses.description: Optional display labels for the four case statuses. Defaults: pass='Passed', fail='Failed', skip='Skipped', flaky='Flaky'. Colour mapping is fixed by the foundation (pass=--ok, fail=--risk, skip=neutral, flaky=--hold) and is NOT configurable.

suites.description: The suite tree as a flat node list with optional parentId references (a suite with no parentId is a root). The renderer builds the collapsible tree; pass/fail/skip/flaky roll-ups per suite are COMPUTED from the cases that belong to the suite and all its descendant suites — do not store counts here. There is also an implicit 'All' root the tree offers above the explicit roots.

id.description: Stable suite identifier referenced by cases[].suiteId and by child suites' parentId, e.g. 'api'.

name.description: Display name of the suite / subsuite, e.g. 'API' or 'Auth endpoints'.

parentId.description: Optional id of the parent suite; omit / null for a root suite. Must reference an existing suite id.

cases.description: Every test case across all suites. Each case belongs to exactly one suite (suiteId) and carries a per-run results map plus a recent run-history sparkline. Suite roll-ups, the donut and pass-rate are derived from cases[].results[activeRunId].

suiteId.description: id of the owning suite (must reference an existing suite). The case rolls up into that suite and its ancestors.

name.description: Human-readable case title, e.g. 'rejects expired access token'.

owner.description: Optional owner / author handle, e.g. '@a.lee' or a team name.

tags.description: Optional free-form labels rendered as mono chips (e.g. 'smoke', 'p0', 'auth', 'slow'). Brand-neutral.

history.description: Recent outcomes of THIS case across the last N runs (oldest → newest), rendered as the tiny last-N-runs sparkline of ok/risk/neutral squares. Independent of the runs[] array so a long tail can be shown compactly. Use only pass/fail/skip here (flaky collapses to its dominant outcome in history).

results.description: Per-run outcome for this case, keyed by run.id. Every run.id in runs[] SHOULD have an entry; a missing entry renders as 'skip' (not executed in that run). At minimum the activeRunId must be present.

additionalProperties.description: This case's outcome in one specific run.

status.description: Outcome of the case in this run. pass=--ok, fail=--risk, skip=neutral/--ink-faint, flaky=--hold (passed on retry).

durationMs.description: Wall-clock duration of this case in this run, in milliseconds. 0 / omitted for skipped cases.

retries.description: Optional retry count in this run; > 0 typically accompanies a 'flaky' status.

failure.description: Failure drill-down for this run's result; present when status is 'fail' or 'flaky'. Rendered in the expandable failure-detail panel under the case row.

message.description: Short human-readable failure / assertion message shown at the top of the detail panel.

stack.description: Optional raw stack trace / log excerpt, rendered verbatim in a dark mono block. Newlines preserved.

steps.description: Optional ordered step list with a per-step status, showing where in the scenario the case stopped.

name.description: Step description, e.g. 'POST /v1/transfers with expired token'.

attachments.description: Attachment REFERENCES only — names / labels of artefacts (screenshots, logs, HARs). NO external URLs or files are loaded; these render as inert mono chips.

name.description: Artefact file name or label, e.g. 'response.json' or 'screenshot-final.png'.

kind.description: Optional artefact kind for the icon hint.

failure.stack:

    at auth.spec.ts:64:18
